value.setup: Cushing draws five straight weeks into critical tank-bottom territory.
value.rationale: Cushing fell 1.08 MB to 18.96 MB, down from 23.02 MB on May 22 — a relentless five-week draw. Tightening hub stocks pressure prompt physical supply, supporting a steeper backwardation in the front WTI spread. Watch for stocks approaching operational minimums (~18 MB) as the catalyst; risk is a Cushing rebuild from rising PADD2 utilization easing off 100%.

value.setup: Large crude draw with falling exports and rising imports signals real tightness.
value.rationale: US crude stocks fell 6.09 MB to 412.1 MB, led by a 3.98 MB PADD3 draw, despite crude exports dropping to 4.93 from 5.07 MbD and imports rising to 5.75 MbD. The draw came on lower refiner inputs (-0.08 MbD), implying genuine supply tightness rather than demand pull. Risk is refinery run cuts continuing to soften crude offtake.

value.setup: Gasoline builds against weakening demand entering peak season.
value.rationale: Gasoline stocks rose 2.06 MB to 216.3 MB while 4-week demand slipped to 8.83 from 8.90 MbD. The counter-seasonal build with softening implied demand undercuts gasoline margins. Catalyst risk: PADD1 utilization collapsed 10.6 points to 76.5%, which could tighten East Coast supply and squeeze the crack higher.

value.setup: Distillate stocks build sharply led by PADD3, pressuring margins.
value.rationale: Distillate inventories jumped 3.06 MB to 106.1 MB, with PADD3 building 2.96 MB, while distillate demand sits at just 3.63 MbD. The surplus barrels weigh on diesel cracks heading into summer. Risk is sustained PADD1 distillate draws (-2.12) and the PADD1 utilization plunge tightening Northeast heating supply.
